---
title: "Welcome!"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: profile.jpg
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/pawel-czyz
    - icon: stack-overflow
      text: StackExchange
      href: https://math.stackexchange.com/users/551592/pawe%C5%82-czy%C5%BC
    - icon: file-person
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=7rzyLvYAAAAJ
    - icon: send
      text: Contact
      href: mailto:pczyz@protonmail.com
---

My name is Paweł Czyż, and I am a Bayesian statistician and machine learning researcher focused on modeling complex data.

I enjoy working on:

  - all aspects Bayesian statistics, from principled workflows and model validation to scalable inference,
  - probabilistic machine learning, especially deep generative models and representation learning,
  - applications in biostatistics and computational biology, including mixed models, survival analysis, and cancer genomics.

Beyond my core research, I am interested in connections between category theory, algebraic topology, differential geometry, and mathematical physics.

I hold a Doctor of Sciences degree from ETH Zurich and a Master’s in Mathematical and Theoretical Physics from the University of Oxford.
